critic_raw:
Issue 1 — Causal Scope Jump: Prediction to Intervention
A model with AUROC 0.81 at predicting churn does not imply that acting on that prediction reduces churn. These are distinct claims. The proposal uses the retrospective prediction performance to justify an intervention program, but the intervention's efficacy depends on: (a) whether discounts cause retention in this population, (b) whether the model-identified population responds to discounts, and (c) whether discounts are cost-effective. None of these are evaluated. The historical observation that discount recipients had lower churn is an observational association from a non-representative, agent-selected group — it does not establish (a), (b), or (c).

Issue 2 — Non-Representative Historical Treatment Assignment
The historical discount assignment was driven by agent judgment (complaints, competitive threats). This creates a correlation between treatment assignment and unobserved customer characteristics (responsiveness to offers, severity of competitive threat) that the model does not capture. The customers the model scores in the top decile are those predicted to churn based on behavioral features; they may or may not be the customers who respond to discounts. Applying the historical discount-churn association to model-selected customers assumes the populations are exchangeable, which is not established.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 — Analysis (no critic visible):
Issue 1: Valid. Prediction accuracy is not intervention efficacy. This is a common scope jump in applied ML proposals. The AUROC tells us the model ranks customers by churn probability; it says nothing about whether contacting those customers with discounts reduces churn. The historical discount data is explicitly from non-random assignment.
Issue 2: Valid. The historical treatment group is selected by agent judgment, not by model score. Applying findings from an agent-selected group to a model-selected group requires assuming the groups are similar on treatment-response dimensions, which is not established.

Pass 2 — Verdicts: Both conceded. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_abandonment_offer_301 — Isolated Debate Run 2

Consistent with Run 1. Both issues conceded by defender operating independently of critic. The prediction-intervention scope jump is the core problem.

Verdict: CRITIQUE_WINS
